summary.intent: Fix three P2 code review findings on a PR implementing a timer component in ars-ui: clamp the progress value, add type="button" to trigger elements, and replace raw format! calls with locale-aware IntlBackend::format_segment_digits for digit formatting.
summary.outcome: All three findings were resolved, intl_backend was threaded into the timer Context following the time_field precedent, 49 tests pass, clippy is clean, and the xci-fast gate passes. The spec was updated to reflect the new API surface.

repo: time_field is the canonical precedent for threading Arc<dyn IntlBackend> through a component Context — it stores the arc directly and uses manual Debug (prints placeholder) and manual PartialEq (omits intl_backend field) to satisfy Machine trait bounds.
Machine trait requires Context: Clone + Debug only, not PartialEq, so PartialEq impls are for internal use and can safely omit non-comparable fields like Arc<dyn IntlBackend>.
context_changed is a transition flag rather than a whole-Context equality check, so omitting intl_backend from PartialEq does not affect state-machine correctness.
StubIntlBackend::format_segment_digits is implemented as format!("{value:0width$}"), meaning existing snapshots are unaffected when the stub is used in tests.

Foundation architecture doc states 'Non-date-time components ignore intl_backend', but a component's own spec can override this — timer §4.1 explicitly requires locale-aware digit formatting, creating a documented tension.
A LocalizedDigitsBackend test double already exists in time_field/tests.rs and can be reused for locale-aware digit tests in other components.
HTML trigger buttons require explicit attr::type_("button") to prevent default form-submission behavior; this is a recurrent finding pattern in the codebase.

code.finding: Added intl_backend: Arc<dyn IntlBackend> to Context, manual Debug/PartialEq impls omitting that field, updated init() signature, fixed formatted_time to call format_segment_digits, and added .clamp() on the progress value.
Updated §1.3, §1.6, §1.7, and §2 anatomy table to document intl_backend threading and type="button" as part of the component API.

workflow: When a foundation-level doc conflicts with a component-level spec, the component spec takes precedence for that component — but the decision should be explicit and the user/owner must confirm before implementing.

Snapshot tests for trigger elements needed bulk updates (7 files) solely due to the type="button" attribute addition; mass snapshot updates are a reliable signal that the change is structural rather than behavioral.

Verifying StubIntlBackend behavior before implementation prevents unnecessary snapshot churn — confirming it produces the same zero-padded output as the raw format! macro meant no snapshot regeneration was needed for the intl_backend threading.

friction: Spec tension between the foundation architecture doc ('non-date-time components ignore intl_backend') and timer's own spec (§4.1 requires locale-aware digits) required a user decision before implementation could proceed, blocking automated resolution.
Seven snapshot files required bulk updates due to the type="button" attribute change, creating a high-volume but low-signal diff.
Adding intl_backend to Context required a follow-up edit to init() — the compiler caught the missing field, but the two-step edit added friction.
open_items: Foundation architecture doc should be updated to note the intl_backend exception for timer, or the rule reworded to 'components that do not display time values'.
Audit other specialized (non-date-time) components that display numeric values for latent locale-aware digit formatting requirements similar to timer §4.1.
LocalizedDigitsBackend test double is duplicated across time_field/tests.rs and timer/tests.rs — evaluate promoting it to a shared test-utilities crate.
